Hero.title: Support for Parents
Hero.description: Navigating education, health, and care systems can feel overwhelming. Obimi gives you the guidance, resources, and community you need to support your child with confidence.

WhyObimi.title: Why Obimi
WhyObimi.description: Obimi was created by people who understand the journey firsthand. We combine lived experience with practical support to help you navigate every step with clarity and confidence.
WhyObimi.videoDescription: Hear from our founder and community about how Obimi is making a difference.
WhyObimi.value1Description: Built from real experience, so you get support that truly understands your challenges.
WhyObimi.value2Description: We break down complex systems into simple steps you can actually follow.
WhyObimi.value3Description: From education to health to social care, everything you need is in one place.
WhyObimi.value4Description: Connect with others who understand your journey and support you every step of the way.

HowWeHelp.title: How We Can Help
HowWeHelp.description: Practical support across every part of your journey.
HowWeHelp.service1Description: From EHCP processes to school placement and reviews, we help you advocate for your child's education.
HowWeHelp.service2Description: Understand diagnoses, work with therapists, and manage appointments with clarity and confidence.
HowWeHelp.service3Description: Get help accessing DLA, Carer's Allowance, and local authority support.
HowWeHelp.service4Description: Connect with other parents, join safe spaces, and take care of your own wellbeing too.
HowWeHelp.service5Description: Training and speaking engagements to support professionals and organisations working with SEND families.

KnowledgeHub.mainSearchPlaceholder: Search for guides, articles, or topics...

KnowledgeHub.categoryEducationDesc: Guidance on EHCPs, school support, and navigating the education system.
KnowledgeHub.categoryHealthDesc: Understand diagnoses, therapies, and how to support your child's development.
KnowledgeHub.catArticle1Title: Annual Reviews of EHCPs: What Parents Need to Know
KnowledgeHub.catArticle1Desc: A simple guide to understanding annual reviews and how to prepare effectively.
KnowledgeHub.catArticle2Title: Education, Health and Care Plans (EHCPs): A Step-by-Step Guide
KnowledgeHub.catArticle2Desc: Navigating the Education, Health and Care Plan process from start to finish.
KnowledgeHub.catArticle3Desc: How schools provide additional support for children with special educational needs.
KnowledgeHub.catArticle4Title: Choosing the Right School for Your Child
KnowledgeHub.catArticle4Desc: Factors to consider when looking for a school that meets your child's unique needs.
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideIntro: For some children, SEN Support may not be enough. An Education, Health and Care Plan (EHCP) provides legally protected support to help them achieve the best possible outcomes.
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideSec1C1: An EHCP is a legal document for children and young people aged 0–25 who need additional support beyond what schools can provide.
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideSec1C2: It outlines your child's needs, the outcomes they're working towards, the support required, and the school or setting.
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideSec1C3: Key point: The support in an EHCP must be delivered by law.
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideSec2C1: Step 1: Request an Assessment - Parents, schools, or professionals can apply. Submit a written request to your local authority. Decision must be made within 6 weeks.
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideSec2C2: Step 2: Needs Assessment - Evidence gathered from parents, school, and health professionals to determine if an EHCP is needed.
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideSec2C3: Step 3: Draft EHCP - You receive a draft plan. You have 15 days to review and request changes. You can choose a school.
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideSec2C4: Step 4: Final Plan - Must be issued within 20 weeks. Becomes legally binding.
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideSec3C1: Needs: What challenges exist for your child.
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideSec3C2: Outcomes: What your child should achieve with the support.
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideSec3C3: Provision: What specific support is provided (therapies, equipment, etc.).
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideSec3C4: Placement: Where the support happens (school or setting).
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideSec4C1: EHCPs are reviewed yearly to ensure support remains relevant. Adjustments can be made as needs change.
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideSec4C2: If a request is refused, you have the right to appeal, request mediation, or seek support from organisations.
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideSec5C1: Be clear and specific about your child's needs.
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideSec5C2: Keep detailed records of all communications and assessments.
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideSec5C3: Push for detailed support descriptions in the plan.
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideSec5C4: Know your rights and don't be afraid to advocate for your child.
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideTakeaway: An EHCP gives your child access to structured, legally protected support. Understanding the process helps you secure the help your child needs.
KnowledgeHub.ehcpGuideCtaText: Need help applying for an EHCP?
KnowledgeHub.healthArticle1Title: Understanding Common SEND Diagnoses: What Parents Need to Know
KnowledgeHub.healthArticle1Desc: A guide to help you understand what a diagnosis means and how to support your child.
KnowledgeHub.healthArticle2Title: Navigating Therapy and Support Services (SALT, OT, CAMHS)
KnowledgeHub.healthArticle2Desc: A simple breakdown of different therapies and services to help you make informed decisions.
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideIntro: Receiving a diagnosis for your child can bring a mix of emotions — relief, confusion, and sometimes fear. This guide is here to help you understand what a diagnosis means, what to expect next, and how to support your child with confidence.
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideSec1C1: A diagnosis helps identify the specific challenges your child may face and opens the door to the right support. It can:
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideSec1C2: Explain your child’s behaviours or difficulties
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideSec1C4: Give access to support services and interventions
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideSec1Alert: A diagnosis does not define your child — it helps support them.
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideSec2C1: Autism (ASD): Affects communication, social interaction, and behaviour. Children may prefer routines and find change difficult.
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideSec2C2: ADHD: Impacts attention, focus, and impulse control. Children may be very active or easily distracted.
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideSec2C3: Speech & Language Difficulties: Challenges with understanding or expressing language. May affect communication and learning.
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideSec2C4: Sensory Processing Difficulties: Over- or under-sensitive to sounds, textures, lights, etc. Can affect behaviour and comfort.
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideSec2Alert: Every child is different — no two experiences are the same.
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideSec3C1: After diagnosis, you may: Be referred to specialists (e.g., therapists)
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideSec3C2: Work with your child’s school for support
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideSec3C3: Explore SEN Support or an EHCP
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideSec3C4: Receive guidance on strategies at home
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideSec3Alert: It’s a process — not everything happens at once.
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideSec4C1: Therapies: Speech and Language Therapy (SALT), Occupational Therapy (OT), Behavioural support
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideSec4C2: School Support: SEN Support plans, Classroom adjustments, One-to-one support
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideSec4C3: Health Services: Paediatricians, CAMHS (mental health support)
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideSec6C1: Caring for a child with additional needs can be overwhelming. Make sure you:
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideTakeaway: A diagnosis is not the end — it’s the beginning of understanding your child better and getting the right support in place.
KnowledgeHub.diagGuideCtaText: Need help understanding your child’s needs or next steps?
KnowledgeHub.therapiesGuideIntro: After a diagnosis, many families are introduced to different therapies and services — but understanding who does what can feel confusing. This guide breaks it down in simple terms.
KnowledgeHub.therapiesGuideSec1C1: Speech and Language Therapy (SALT): Helps children with communication, speech, and understanding language.
KnowledgeHub.therapiesGuideSec1C2: Occupational Therapy (OT): Supports motor skills, coordination, and everyday activities like writing or dressing.
KnowledgeHub.therapiesGuideSec1C3: CAMHS: Provides mental health support for children and young people.
KnowledgeHub.therapiesGuideSec3Alert: This is normal — you’re not alone.
KnowledgeHub.therapiesGuideTakeaway: Understanding therapy services helps you make informed decisions and support your child more effectively.
KnowledgeHub.therapiesGuideCtaText: Need help navigating therapy and support services?

Community.title: You Don't Have to
Community.description: Raising a child with SEND comes with unique challenges — but you don't have to face them by yourself. Join a community of parents who understand, share, and support each other every step of the way.

MediaSection.description: Glimpses of our community and the impact we're making together.
MediaSection.video1Description: Watch our latest update on community impact.

MediaSection.video3Description: Watch our latest update on expert advice.

Mission.title: Our Mission
Mission.heroDescription: At Obimi, our mission is to support, empower, and connect parents and caregivers of children with additional needs.
Mission.quote: Raising a child with SEND is deeply meaningful, but it can also be overwhelming and isolating. That's why we place parent wellbeing at the heart of everything we do.
Mission.floatingBadge: When parents are supported, families thrive.
Mission.sectionDescription: We provide holistic support across every stage of your journey.
Mission.closingMessage: At Obimi, we remind every parent: your wellbeing matters too. Supporting you means strengthening the entire family.
Mission.visionDescription: A world where every SEND parent and caregiver feels confident, informed, and supported — able to advocate for their child and thrive as a family.
Mission.block1Description: Safe spaces, peer groups, and tools to help you take care of yourself.
Mission.block2Description: Clear, easy-to-understand resources on SEND processes and your rights.
Mission.block3Description: Standing with you as you navigate complex systems.
Mission.block4Description: Bringing parents together so no one feels alone.

Support.title: Support Obimi.
Support.heroDescription: Your support helps parents and caregivers navigate the SEND journey with confidence, clarity, and community.
Support.quote: Obimi exists to ensure no parent has to walk that journey alone.
Support.impactTitle: How Your Support Makes a Difference
Support.impactDescription: Every contribution directly impacts the lives of SEND families globally.
Support.donationDescription: Your contribution directly supports families and keeps our resources accessible.

Support.finalTitle: Together, we can ensure no parent walks the SEND journey alone.
Support.impact1Description: Helping parents understand their rights and take action.
Support.impact2Description: Providing spaces where parents can connect and share.
Support.impact3Description: Bringing families and experts together globally.

CommunityPage.description: The SEND journey can feel isolating, but you don’t have to go through it alone. At Obimi, you’re part of a supportive community that understands, listens, and cares.
CommunityPage.offer1Description: Our moderated members-only forum gives you a place to ask questions, share experiences, and speak freely in a supportive environment.
CommunityPage.offer2Description: Join our relaxed online coffee mornings and connect with other parents from the comfort of your home.
CommunityPage.offer3Description: Meet other parents and caregivers face-to-face, build real connections, and grow your support network.
CommunityPage.offer4Description: Learn from real experiences — from practical tips to everyday advice that makes your journey easier.
CommunityPage.midCtaTitle: You don’t have to figure everything out alone.
CommunityPage.depthDescription: Our community is built on empathy, respect, and shared experience — a place where every parent feels seen, heard, and supported.
CommunityPage.finalCtaTitle: Join a Community That Gets It

ContactPage.description: Have a question or need support? We’re here to help — reach out and we’ll get back to you.
ContactPage.contactInfoDescription: We'd love to hear from you. Whether you have a question about our services, community, or just want to say hello, our team is ready to support you.
ContactPage.formDescription: Fill out the form below and we'll get back to you shortly.

FAQPage.description: Quick answers to help you understand how Obimi works and how we can support you.
FAQPage.faq1Answer: SEND stands for Special Educational Needs and Disabilities. It refers to children who need additional support in learning, development, or daily life.
FAQPage.faq2Answer: Obimi supports parents, carers, and guardians raising children with SEND — whether you’re just starting out or already navigating the system.
FAQPage.faq3Question: What kind of support does Obimi provide?
FAQPage.faq3Answer: We offer guidance across education, health, and social care, including one-to-one support, workshops, resources, and community connection.
FAQPage.faq4Question: Do I need to pay for support?
FAQPage.faq4Answer: Some services are free, while others (like personalised sessions) may have a cost. We aim to keep support accessible for all families.
FAQPage.faq5Question: How do I join the community?
FAQPage.faq5Answer: You can join our community through our platform to connect with other parents, access support, and take part in events.
FAQPage.faq6Question: I’m new to SEND — where should I start?
FAQPage.faq6Answer: Start with our Knowledge Hub for simple guides, or reach out for support — we’ll help you take the next step.
FAQPage.faq7Question: Does Obimi replace legal or medical advice?
FAQPage.faq7Answer: No. We provide guidance and support, but we do not replace professional legal or medical services.
FAQPage.faq8Question: Can I get involved or share my story?
FAQPage.faq8Answer: Yes. You can volunteer, partner with us, or share your experience to support others in the community.
FAQPage.faq9Answer: You can reach us through our contact page or email. We’re here to help.
FAQPage.finalCtaDescription: We're here to help. Reach out to us and we'll get back to you as soon as possible.

ServicesPage.description: Practical, empathetic support designed to meet you wherever you are on your SEND journey.
ServicesPage.service1Text: Feeling stuck with applications, school decisions, or navigating the system? Our one-to-one sessions provide personalised guidance to help you move forward with clarity.
ServicesPage.service2Text: Join practical, interactive sessions led by experts and experienced parents, with space to ask questions and learn in real time.
ServicesPage.service3Text: Access a growing library of clear, practical resources designed to help you understand your rights and take action with confidence.
ServicesPage.service4Text: Connect with parents who understand your journey. Share experiences, ask questions, and find support in a safe, welcoming space.
ServicesPage.finalCtaDescription: Whether you’re just starting out or navigating complex challenges, Obimi is here to support you every step of the way.

Footer.subText: Empowering parents and caregivers through expert guidance, community support, and practical resources.
